$comment: Catalog of selectable aka-claude-tools additions. Drives the installer menu and documents the payload. 'recommended' additions are pre-selected at the install prompt.

additions.name: Secure base settings
additions.prompt: Secure base settings (deny credential reads, telemetry off)?
additions.description: Deny-reads on credential paths (~/.ssh, ~/.aws, .env, keychains, …) and disables nonessential telemetry/feedback traffic. Always safe to layer on.

additions.name: Web guard
additions.prompt: Web guard (block secrets in web queries + outbound Bash)?
additions.description: PreToolUse guard on WebSearch/WebFetch, plus outbound Bash commands (fast-gated: only commands containing curl/wget/nc/… are scanned, ~98% of Bash calls pay ~0 ms). Blocks queries/commands containing detected secrets (trufflehog), token/SSH-key shapes, and — if configured — your org's internal identifiers.

additions.name: Bash guard
additions.prompt: Bash guard — block credential exfil + curl|bash in Bash [bun]?
additions.description: PreToolUse guard on Bash. Denies credential exfil (key shape + outbound tool) and pipe-to-shell (curl | bash); alerts on nc/socat/sendmail/env-dump/inline-interpreter. Requires bun; skipped at install if bun is absent.

additions.name: RTK saver
additions.prompt: RTK saver — rewrite commands to compact rtk output for token savings [needs rtk]?
additions.description: PreToolUse rewrite on Bash. Transparently rewrites git/gh/cat/ls/find/npm/docker/etc. to compact `rtk` equivalents for token savings. Self-skips at runtime if rtk/jq are absent, so it's inert until you install rtk. Also installs a strictly read-only rtk allowlist (rtk-allowlist.json) so rewritten read commands don't add prompt friction; mutating/egress forms still prompt.

additions.name: Responsive status line
additions.description: Width-adaptive status line (git, context, usage). Resolves its own config dir, so it works in any isolated config folder.

additions.name: /wrap-up command
additions.description: Repo-agnostic end-of-session handoff that PREPARES, never executes: summarize, verify (stop on failure), stage intentionally with a secret/artifact scan (or just propose the git add commands), draft a conventional commit message. Multi-user aware: leaves others' staged changes alone, stops over in-progress rebases, proposes a feature branch instead of working on main. Never commits, merges, or pushes unless explicitly asked.

additions.name: loop-designer skill
additions.prompt: loop-designer skill (compile a spec into a minimal autonomous-run prompt)?
additions.description: Skill that compiles a finished spec/plan (any doc with verifiable criteria, constraints, and human gates) into the smallest kickoff prompt that runs it well in a fresh orchestrator session — must-have discipline core + an evidence-based catalog of opt-in guardrails distilled from real multi-hour autonomous runs. Includes a Harden mode that slims hand-written kickoff prompts. Pure prompt/skill files — no hooks, no runtime cost.

additions.name: secure-deep-research workflow
additions.prompt: secure-deep-research workflow (privacy-aware multi-source research, sensitive-topic gating)?
additions.description: Privacy-aware deep-research workflow: fan-out web search -> fetch -> 3-vote adversarial verify -> cited synthesis. The Scope agent classifies privacy sensitivity (conservative); sensitive topics are query-redacted, GATED for confirmation before any external query fires, fan-out-reduced, and routed through a self-hosted SearXNG MCP server (mcp__searxng__*). Normal topics use WebSearch/WebFetch at full fan-out. Model-tiered: Scope+Synthesize inherit the session model, the Search/Fetch/Verify fan-out runs on Sonnet. Placed in <dir>/workflows/ and auto-registers as the /secure-deep-research skill. NOTE: the sensitive-topic path needs a self-hosted SearXNG MCP server configured; without it, only the normal (WebSearch) path works.

additions.name: Harness pointer (opt-in)
additions.description: PreToolUse guard on Bash that intercepts org-configured commands and points the agent to the right approach for your environment (e.g. gh -> git). Guidance, not a security boundary. Ships OFF and empty — most engineers want every CLI. Enable + configure via aka-claude-tools.config.

additions.name: Shell startup audit skill
additions.prompt: Shell startup audit skill (aliases, secrets, persistence, git drift)?
additions.description: Read-only auditor + skill. Walks your shell rc and every file it sources (cycle-safe) and flags: hardcoded credentials (values redacted), persistence-suspicious patterns (pipe-to-shell, eval-of-fetched, DYLD/LD_PRELOAD, writes into other rc files), duplicate or dangling aliases, and git-baseline drift. A detective tripwire for the shell-startup slice — nothing is modified; not a substitute for launchd/cron/ssh review.

additions.name: Startup-write guard
additions.prompt: Startup-write guard — block Bash writes to shell rc files [persistence]?
additions.description: PreToolUse guard on Bash. Blocks commands that write/append/redirect into shell startup files (~/.zshrc, ~/.zshenv, ~/.bashrc, ~/.profile, …), closing the gap the secure-settings Edit/Write deny leaves open: a Bash `echo >> ~/.zshrc` redirection bypasses that deny. Reads are unaffected; intentional edits go through your own shell. Guidance, not a hard boundary.
